sutta: protalia the wanderer came to visit the exalted 1 and when coming to him greeted him courteously etc sat down to 1 side etc as he said at 1 side the exalted 1 said this to protagona the wanderer potalia these 4 persons are found existing in the world what for here in puttalia a certain person speaks in dispraise of what deserves not praise seasonably saying what is a fact and true but he does not speak in praise of what deserves praise in like manner again battalia a certain person speaks in praise of what deserves it but not in disgrace of what deserves not praise saying seasonably 1 is a fact and true yet again battalion a certain person speaks neither in dispraise of what deserves not praise nor in praise of the praiseworthy saying seasonably what is a fact and true once more battalia a certain person speaks both in displays of what deserves not praise and in praise of what is praiseworthy saying seasonably what is a fact and true these potalia are the 4 persons found existing in the world now pratalya of these 4 which person in your view is to be accounted most admirable and rare and then pratalya said of these 4 persons master gotama he who speaks neither in this praise of what deserves not praise nor in praise of the praiseworthy saying seasonably what is a fact and true this person in my view is to be accounted most admirable and rare of these 4 why so because master gautama his indifference is admirable and the buddha said now potalia these 4 persons found existing in the world of these 4 persons pratalya i maintain that he who speaks in this praise of what deserves not praise and in praise of what is praiseworthy saying seasonably what is a fact and true he is the most admirable and rare why so because potalia his discrimination of proper occasions is admirable and prutalia said well master gautama these 4 persons are found in the world of these 4 master gautama he who speaks in this praise of what deserves not praise and in praise of the praiseworthy saying seasonably what is a fact and true he as you say is the most admirable and rare why so because master gautama his discrimination of proper occasions is admirable it is excellent master gautama it is wonderful master gotama just as if master gautama 1 should raise the fallen or show forth the hidden or point the way to him that wanders astray a hold up a light in the darkness so that they who have eyes may see objects even so in diverse ways has dhamma been set forth by the wordy gotama i do go for refuge to the worthy gotama may the worthy gotama accept me as a lay disciple from this day forth as long as life lasts as 1 who has gone to refuge who has gone to him for refuge now this uh quite an interesting suta about these 4 persons uh 1 he speaks in displays of the what is bad now if what what is uh what is blameable he says it is blameable what is praiseworthy he does not praise that's the first 1 second 1 he preach he gives praise to what deserves praise but he does not blame put blame on what is blameable and the third 1 he neither praises or blames her and the fourth 1 he both blames what deserves blame and praises what deserves praise now of these 4 the buddha says the last 1 is most admirable sometimes people may have a a different opinion now if you did not study this sometimes you would think oh it is good to praise somebody if he deserves praise but if he is blame worthy we should not blame him we should not say bad luck not talk bad about somebody but here the buddha is saying that we should speak very straightforwardly what what is true or what is um worthy of praise we praise what is worthy of blame we blame but however we must consider very carefully whether it is the weather what is our motive in speaking is it a good motive or is it a bad motive is it going to do any harm to yourself or is going to do any harm to another person and whether we are speaking at the right time etc so they are certain these are the certain things that we should consider when we praise or we blame but the buddha is trying to tell us that it is good to speak straight 11.101 on a certain occasion the exalted 1 was staying near savarti and jeter grove in anata pindika spa then the exalted 1 addressed the monk saying monks yes lord replied those monks to the exalted 1 the exalted 1 said this monks there are these 4 rain clouds what for the thunderer not the rainer the rainer not the thunderer the neither thunderer norena the both thunderer and rayner these are the 4 in like men amongst these 4 persons resembling rain clouds ati beef are to be found existing in the world what for the thundra and not the rainer the reina and not the thunderer the neither thunderer nor reina the both thunderer and rayner these are the 4 now monks how is a person a thunderer but no reigner in this case monks a person speaks but acts not thus he is 1 who thunders but reigns not this is that cloud thunders but rains not so using this figure do i speak of this person and how monks is a person a reigner but no thunderer in this case monks a person is 1 who acts but speaks not thus he is 1 who reigns but thunders not just as that cloud using this figure do i speak of this person and how monks is a person 1 who is neither thunderer nor rainer in this case a person neither speaks nor acts thus he is neither thunderer nor rainer and how monks is a person both thunderer and reiner in this case a person both speaks and acts thus he is both tandara and rayner so these are the 4 persons found existing in the world this 1 of the sutas where you can see the buddha even even when he's speaking dhamma he's a bit humorous thunderer is a person now who speaks a lot and and the rainer is the person who acts like so there are certain people now they speak but they don't act some people they act but they don't speak and that's people and don't speak and don't act then some people both act and both speak now so we we we can see this in other persons but it's good sometimes to examine ourselves and see what kind of person we are another suitable monks there are these 4 pots what for the closed and empty the open and full the open and empty and the closed and full pot in like manner monks there are these 4 persons resembling pots to be found existing in the world what for the closed and empty the open and full the open and empty and the closed and full and now monks how is a person closed and empty in this case the looks of a certain person is charming whether he be departing or approaching looking forward or backward bending or unbending bearing outer rope or ball and row but he understands not as it really is the meaning of this is dukkha this is the arising of dukkha this is the seizing of dukkha this is the path that leads to the seizing of dukkha thus monks a person is closed and empty just as that pond which is closed and empty even so using this figure do i speak of this person
